articles.title: Mouni Roy's husband Suraj Nambiar denies 'malicious' separation claims
articles.description: Suraj Nambiar denies rumours of alimony disputes and third-party involvement in his separation from Mouni Roy. He called the reports 'malicious' and stated the decision to part ways was mutual, amicable, and with respect for each other.

articles.title: Biggest US & NATO disaster? Russia’s bombers now carry more nuclear...
articles.description: Russia is reportedly pushing ahead with a major modernization of its Tu-160 strategic bomber fleet as it waits for the future PAK DA stealth bomber to arrive. Reports say the upgraded Tu-160M is being treated as a bridge platform meant to keep Russia’s long-range bomber force credible through improvements in avionics, navigation, autopilot systems, electronics, and weapons integration.

articles.title: ‘Cockroach Janta Party’ goes viral after CJI remark; Mahua Moitra, Kirti Azad join satirical trend
articles.description: The ‘Cockroach Janta Party’ has gone viral after CJI Surya Kant’s controversial remark sparked backlash online. TMC MPs Mahua Moitra and Kirti Azad have now joined the satirical movement on X.

articles.title: 'No namaz on roads': CM Yogi Adityanath suggests shift system for worship; says no right to disrupt public movement
articles.description: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath stated that offering namaz on public roads is not permissible, as it disrupts traffic and inconveniences the public. He emphasized that roads are for movement and suggested organized religious practices in designated places or in shifts to maintain public order and normal life.

articles.title: ‘Team scapegoat’: Introvert worker says manager purposefully gives incomplete instructions to blame her when things go wrong while ‘star employee’ wins office politics by ‘fake laughing’ at boss’s jokes
articles.description: A new employee feels scapegoated at work due to a manager providing incomplete instructions and blaming newer hires for problems. The situation is worsened by perceived favoritism towards a senior employee and communication issues with older freelancers. Reddit users advise documenting everything and actively seeking new employment.

articles.title: Eye on China? Defence ties in focus as Rajnath Singh visits Vietnam amid Xi’s Indo-Pacific dares
articles.description: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh is on a key visit to Vietnam aimed at strengthening India-Vietnam defence ties. The visit focuses on reviewing ongoing defence cooperation, including capacity building, joint production, maritime security, and strategic collaboration in the Indo-Pacific. India and Vietnam, which recently elevated ties to an Enhanced Comprehensive Strategic Partnership, are also expected to discuss future defence projects and regional stability. With growing convergence on security issues, the visit underscores Vietnam’s importance in India’s Act East policy and both nations’ shared commitment to peace, prosperity, and regional security.

articles.title: VD Satheesan hugs Rahul Gandhi after taking oath as Kerala CM, Chennithala also part of cabinet
articles.description: V. D. Satheesan officially took oath as the new Chief Minister of Kerala following the Congress-led UDF’s sweeping electoral victory. Satheesan and 20 cabinet ministers were sworn in by Governor Rajendra Vishwanath Arlekar in the presence of top leaders, including Mallikarjun Kharge, Rahul Gandhi, and Priyanka Gandhi Vadra. Alliance partners, including the Indian Union Muslim League, were also represented in the cabinet. The UDF secured a commanding 102 seats in the 140-member Kerala Assembly.

articles.title: Kerala CM Satheesan's first order: Free bus travel for women, ₹3,000 hike for ASHA workers
articles.description: The UDF government in Kerala announced free KSRTC bus travel for women, dedicated department for elderly after first Cabinet meeting.

articles.title: PM Modi gets Sweden’s top honour, lauds India-EU ties, delivers strong message on terrorism
articles.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi held key meetings with Ulf Kristersson and Ursula von der Leyen, reinforcing India’s growing ties with Sweden and the European Union. The leaders praised the deepening India-Europe partnership, with discussions focused on trade, innovation, green transition, and strategic cooperation. In a significant diplomatic honour, PM Modi was awarded Sweden’s prestigious Royal Order of the Polar Star, recognizing his efforts to strengthen bilateral relations. The visit highlights India’s expanding global engagement and strategic outreach across Europe.

articles.title: Bengal approves ₹3,000 monthly aid scheme for women, scraps state OBC list
articles.description: The West Bengal cabinet on Monday approved the 'Annapurna' scheme of Rs 3,000 monthly assistance to women from June 1, and allowed free travel for them in government-run buses. Addressing a press conference after the meeting, state minister Agnimitra Paul said women, who applied for citizenship under the CAA and approached tribunals for inclusion in voter rolls, will also receive 'Annapurna' scheme benefits. "The cabinet gave a nod to the 'Annapurna' scheme of Rs 3,000 monthly assistance to women from June 1. It also approved a proposal to allow free travel for women in government-run buses from June 1," the minister said. The government also approved the constitution of the 7th state pay commission for its employees, and staffers in allied statutory civic bodies, education boards and those working in state-run education institutions, she said. "The Bengal cabinet decided to discontinue government assistance to groups based on religious categorisation from June. The government also

articles.title: Angry Israelis HANDCUFF ‘Baby Netanyahu’ In EXPLOSIVE Street Showdown | ‘BLOOD On Our Hands...’
articles.description: Netanyahu is facing a massive crisis at home. Thousands of protesters have completely shut down Habima Square in Tel Aviv, calling the current government a "disaster for the State of Israel." Placards reading "Crime Minister" and "How much blood will be spilled?" filled the air as citizens expressed fury over endless military operations that have failed to stop Hamas or Hezbollah from regrouping. With the leader of the Democrats, Yair Golan, blasting Netanyahu's "extreme nationalism," rumors are swirling that coalition cracks could trigger an early snap election as soon as September. Watch the anger on the ground.

articles.description: Tibetans in exile demand China reveal the whereabouts of the 11th Panchen Lama, abducted in 1995, marking 31 years of enforced disappearance.

articles.title: Iran Threatens "Stormy Scenarios" Targeting U.S. Bases If Trump Resumes Strikes
articles.description: The shadow war between the U.S. and Iran is about to get a lot more dangerous. Following President Trump’s recent warnings that the U.S. is prepared to resume military strikes if backchannel talks fail, Tehran has hit back with its bluntest threat yet. Iranian military spokesman Abolfazl Shekarchi called the American military a "decayed army" and warned Trump that any further "foolishness" will be met with "crushing, stormy, and surprising scenarios." Iran is signaling that the next phase of conflict won't just be a repeat of the last 40-day war—it will target every U.S. base in the region. Watch the breakdown.
